metadata.title:
Mayor Intends to Fire Commissioner as Soon


as Signs arc Right.

pages.raw_text:
II the Division st. asphalt Job,
for which a bill of $6,8s4 Produced
from $7,114) was new work, why
was it not advertised for bids as
provided In tho city charter7


If it comes under the head Of
maintenance work why was It not
done under the maintenance con-
tract rate by which the Barber COM
pany agrees to maintain the street
for 10 years for $2,4oo7


Manager Baylor, Of the asphalt
company, claims the work was
regularly ordered under the main
tenance contract which obviates
calling for bids.


Yet in urging collection Of the
bill charged he does not for q
minute argue that the work done
was maintenance work. The
amount Of the bill rendered the
city proves that without further
remark.


Wherein this asphalt business Is
surely peculiar and merits close in
Quiry. The maintenance contract
appears to be marvel of elasticity,


1t serves as an excuse for order
ing work without competition in a
nice quiet way that is not main
tenance work at ell when 1t comes
to collecting the bill.


The contract calls for mainten-
ance of Division st. pavement for
10 years for $1,000. Maintenance
means repairing wear and tear re-
sulting from use.


A single job ON this same pave
ment handed out under this same
contract calls for payment of $6,884,
and the company's original charge
was nearly times the amount OF
the maintenance contract. The city
dug up the street for water mains
and the company replaced the pave
ment.


The work is ordered under
stated contract provision and col
lected for at any rate the company
considers the job worth. 1t is fish
at the beginning and fowl at the
finish. And not single city COM
missioner understands.


Commissioner ONO, who has
charge OF the city street depart
ment, will be asked no questions
about the matter, II is stated.
